body.p: ExtraSuite document.xml supports rich inline formatting using standard HTML-like tags. Here is a tour of what you can do with text.
Use bold for emphasis, italic for titles and terms, and bold italic for strong emphasis. You can also use underline for links or warnings, and strikethrough to show removed content.
Documents support six heading levels, plus special title and subtitle styles.
Use type="bullet" for unordered lists. Nesting is supported up to level 8.
Use type="decimal" for ordered lists. Great for sequential steps.
body.li: Run pip install extrasuite or use uvx extrasuite
A browser window will open automatically
Log in with your corporate account
Pull your first document and start editing
body.p: Use type="alpha" for lettered lists — useful for options or alternatives.
body.li: Service account token — for Docs, Sheets, Slides
OAuth delegation token — for Gmail and Calendar
Offline mode — for diff without any authentication
body.p: Use type="roman" for formal or legal-style numbering.
Use type="checkbox" for task lists and checklists.
Different list types can be nested inside each other for complex structures.
body.li: push — apply changes to live document
